us_eu_tracker.week_development: No material change — carry forward prior framing.

weekly_brief_narrative: No dominant strategic autonomy signal emerged this week, as comprehensive searches across Tier 1 institutional sources and Tier 2 specialist press returned null results on all six indicators. This quiet period underscores potential monitoring gaps in hybrid operations and foreign interference, where active sourcing from EEAS, Hybrid CoE, and DFRLab is mandated but yielded no hits—flagged per analytical rules as coverage failure rather than event absence. Defence integration remains stable at score 2, with no EDF/PESCO/ReArm updates; energy at 3 absent dependency shifts; tech at 2 without sovereignty advances; trade at 2 sans coercion episodes; diplomatic at 3 pending alliance moves—all anchored to ECFR 2026 calibration baselines without weekly adjustment. Hybrid threat environment showed zero confirmed incidents, no attributed cyber/FIMI/sabotage patterns from Russia/China; episodic flags avoided due to lack of any events. US-EU dynamics neutral, carrying forward prior framing absent transatlantic friction or cooperation points; NATO-EU stable. Watch forthcoming: potential EEAS FIMI reports, Hybrid CoE publications, PESCO project milestones mid-April, US election cycle interference risks, and Q2 sanctions enforcement reviews for trajectory signals.
